questiontext.text:
Create the following SQL Server logins with SQL Server Authentication:
| login | password | | ----- | -------- | | `amara` | `P@zzw0rd` | | `barry` | `P@zzw0rd` | | `clark` | `P@zzw0rd` | | `dante` | `P@zzw0rd` |
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

expected.text:
Dump from 'sys.sql_logins':
name type_desc is_disabled 1 amara SQL_LOGIN False 2 barry SQL_LOGIN False 3 clark SQL_LOGIN False 4 dante SQL_LOGIN False
Checking users connection:
amara: ------- success
(1 rows affected)
barry: ------- success
(1 rows affected)
clark: ------- success
(1 rows affected)
dante: ------- success
(1 rows affected)

extra.text:
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database=None)
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT name, type_desc, is_disabled FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE name IN('amara','barry','clark','dante'); """ print("Dump from 'sys.sql_logins':\n") print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database=None))
print("\nChecking users connection:\n")
CFG['password'] = 'P@zzw0rd'
CFG['user'] = 'amara' clt = ['/opt/mssql-tools/bin/sqlcmd', '-S', 'tcp:'+CFG['server']+','+str(CFG['port']), '-U', CFG['user'], '-P', CFG['password'], '-d', CFG['database'], '-l', '4', '-t', '4', '-b', '-k1', '-r1', '-C', '-i'+DB_SCRIPT_FILENAME] print(invoke_commandline_sql("SELECT 'success' AS 'amara:';", database=None, command_line_tool=clt))
CFG['user'] = 'barry' clt = ['/opt/mssql-tools/bin/sqlcmd', '-S', 'tcp:'+CFG['server']+','+str(CFG['port']), '-U', CFG['user'], '-P', CFG['password'], '-d', CFG['database'], '-l', '4', '-t', '4', '-b', '-k1', '-r1', '-C', '-i'+DB_SCRIPT_FILENAME] print(invoke_commandline_sql("SELECT 'success' AS 'barry:';", database=None, command_line_tool=clt))
CFG['user'] = 'clark' clt = ['/opt/mssql-tools/bin/sqlcmd', '-S', 'tcp:'+CFG['server']+','+str(CFG['port']), '-U', CFG['user'], '-P', CFG['password'], '-d', CFG['database'], '-l', '4', '-t', '4', '-b', '-k1', '-r1', '-C', '-i'+DB_SCRIPT_FILENAME] print(invoke_commandline_sql("SELECT 'success' AS 'clark:';", database=None, command_line_tool=clt))
CFG['user'] = 'dante' clt = ['/opt/mssql-tools/bin/sqlcmd', '-S', 'tcp:'+CFG['server']+','+str(CFG['port']), '-U', CFG['user'], '-P', CFG['password'], '-d', CFG['database'], '-l', '4', '-t', '4', '-b', '-k1', '-r1', '-C', '-i'+DB_SCRIPT_FILENAME] print(invoke_commandline_sql("SELECT 'success' AS 'dante:';", database=None, command_line_tool=clt))

name.text: 02 - create database users mapped to logins

questiontext.text:
Create the following database user accounts in the `important_data` database mapped to existing server logins:
* `amara` * `barry` * `clark`
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

expected.text:
Dump from 'sys.database_principals':
name type_desc authentication_type_desc 1 dbo SQL_USER INSTANCE 2 guest SQL_USER NONE 3 INFORMATION_SCHEMA SQL_USER NONE 4 sys SQL_USER NONE 5 amara SQL_USER INSTANCE 6 barry SQL_USER INSTANCE 7 clark SQL_USER INSTANCE
Checking database user connections:
amara: ------- success
(1 rows affected)
barry: ------- success
(1 rows affected)
clark: ------- success
(1 rows affected)

extra.text:
__db_prepare__ = """ CREATE DATABASE important_data; go USE important_data; go CREATE TABLE cars ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, manufacturer VARCHAR(50), model VARCHAR(50), type VARCHAR(50), horsepower INT, ); go INSERT INTO cars VALUES (3, 'Acura' , 'ILX' , 'Sedan' , 201 ), (7, 'Fiat' , '500X' , 'SUV' , 150 ), (8, 'Lexus' , 'RC' , 'Coupe' , 467 ); go CREATE TABLE houses ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, owner_surname VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), area FLOAT, bedrooms int ); go INSERT INTO houses VALUES (2, 'Markowitz' , '293 Davis Place, Toledo, OH' , 156.2 , 3), (3, 'Earl' , '2071 Rockford Mountain Lane, Appleton, WI' , 54.5 , 1), (5, 'Hampton' , '930 Sunny Glen Lane, Cleveland, OH' , 111.9 , 2); go CREATE LOGIN amara WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN barry WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN clark WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; go ALTER SERVER ROLE dbcreator ADD MEMBER clark; go """
invoke_commandline_sql(__db_prepare__, database=None)
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database='important_data')
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT name, type_desc, authentication_type_desc FROM sys.database_principals WHERE type_desc = 'SQL_USER'; """
print("Dump from 'sys.database_principals':\n") print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database='important_data'))
print("\nChecking database user connections:\n")
CFG['password'] = 'P@zzw0rd' CFG['database'] = 'important_data'
CFG['user'] = 'amara' clt = ['/opt/mssql-tools/bin/sqlcmd', '-S', 'tcp:'+CFG['server']+','+str(CFG['port']), '-U', CFG['user'], '-P', CFG['password'], '-d', CFG['database'], '-l', '4', '-t', '4', '-b', '-k1', '-r1', '-C', '-i'+DB_SCRIPT_FILENAME] print(invoke_commandline_sql("SELECT 'success' AS 'amara:';", database=None, command_line_tool=clt))
CFG['user'] = 'barry' clt = ['/opt/mssql-tools/bin/sqlcmd', '-S', 'tcp:'+CFG['server']+','+str(CFG['port']), '-U', CFG['user'], '-P', CFG['password'], '-d', CFG['database'], '-l', '4', '-t', '4', '-b', '-k1', '-r1', '-C', '-i'+DB_SCRIPT_FILENAME] print(invoke_commandline_sql("SELECT 'success' AS 'barry:';", database=None, command_line_tool=clt))
CFG['user'] = 'clark' clt = ['/opt/mssql-tools/bin/sqlcmd', '-S', 'tcp:'+CFG['server']+','+str(CFG['port']), '-U', CFG['user'], '-P', CFG['password'], '-d', CFG['database'], '-l', '4', '-t', '4', '-b', '-k1', '-r1', '-C', '-i'+DB_SCRIPT_FILENAME] print(invoke_commandline_sql("SELECT 'success' AS 'clark:';", database=None, command_line_tool=clt))

questiontext.text:
Add database user accounts in the `important_data` database to the following roles:
| account | role | | ------- | ---- | | `amara` | `db_owner` | | `barry` | `db_owner` | | `clark` | `db_datareader` and `db_securityadmin` |
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

expected.text: RoleName UserName 1 db_owner dbo 2 db_owner amara 3 db_owner barry 4 db_securityadmin clark 5 db_datareader clark

extra.text:
__db_prepare__ = """ CREATE DATABASE important_data; go USE important_data; go CREATE TABLE cars ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, manufacturer VARCHAR(50), model VARCHAR(50), type VARCHAR(50), horsepower INT, ); go INSERT INTO cars VALUES (3, 'Acura' , 'ILX' , 'Sedan' , 201 ), (7, 'Fiat' , '500X' , 'SUV' , 150 ), (8, 'Lexus' , 'RC' , 'Coupe' , 467 ); go CREATE TABLE houses ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, owner_surname VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), area FLOAT, bedrooms int ); go INSERT INTO houses VALUES (2, 'Markowitz' , '293 Davis Place, Toledo, OH' , 156.2 , 3), (3, 'Earl' , '2071 Rockford Mountain Lane, Appleton, WI' , 54.5 , 1), (5, 'Hampton' , '930 Sunny Glen Lane, Cleveland, OH' , 111.9 , 2); go CREATE LOGIN amara WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN barry WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN clark WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; go ALTER SERVER ROLE dbcreator ADD MEMBER clark; go USE important_data; go CREATE USER amara FOR LOGIN amara; CREATE USER barry FOR LOGIN barry; CREATE USER clark FOR LOGIN clark; go """
invoke_commandline_sql(__db_prepare__, database=None)
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database='important_data')
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT p1.name AS RoleName, p2.name AS UserName FROM sys.database_role_members AS drm JOIN sys.database_principals AS p1 ON drm.role_principal_id = p1.principal_id JOIN sys.database_principals AS p2 ON drm.member_principal_id = p2.principal_id; """
print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database='important_data'))

questiontext.text:
Grant permissions allowing `SELECT` and `INSERT` on the `houses` table in the `important_data` database to user account `barry`.
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

expected.text: entity_name subentity_name permission_name 1 houses SELECT 2 houses INSERT 3 houses id SELECT 4 houses owner_surname SELECT 5 houses address SELECT 6 houses area SELECT 7 houses bedrooms SELECT

questiontext.text:
Grant a **denying** permission for `SELECT` on the `address` column of the `houses` table in the `important_data` database to user account `barry`.
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

question.answer: DENY SELECT ON OBJECT::houses(address) TO barry; go

expected.text: PermissionsForObjects 1 GRANT INSERT ON [dbo].[houses] TO [barry] 2 GRANT SELECT ON [dbo].[houses] TO [barry] PermissionForColumns 1 DENY SELECT ON [dbo].[houses](address) TO [barry]

extra.text:
__db_prepare__ = """ CREATE DATABASE important_data; go USE important_data; go CREATE TABLE cars ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, manufacturer VARCHAR(50), model VARCHAR(50), type VARCHAR(50), horsepower INT, ); go INSERT INTO cars VALUES (3, 'Acura' , 'ILX' , 'Sedan' , 201 ), (7, 'Fiat' , '500X' , 'SUV' , 150 ), (8, 'Lexus' , 'RC' , 'Coupe' , 467 ); go CREATE TABLE houses ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, owner_surname VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), area FLOAT, bedrooms int ); go INSERT INTO houses VALUES (2, 'Markowitz' , '293 Davis Place, Toledo, OH' , 156.2 , 3), (3, 'Earl' , '2071 Rockford Mountain Lane, Appleton, WI' , 54.5 , 1), (5, 'Hampton' , '930 Sunny Glen Lane, Cleveland, OH' , 111.9 , 2); go CREATE LOGIN amara WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN barry WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN clark WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; go ALTER SERVER ROLE dbcreator ADD MEMBER clark; go USE important_data; go CREATE USER amara FOR LOGIN amara; CREATE USER barry FOR LOGIN barry; CREATE USER clark FOR LOGIN clark; go ALTER ROLE db_owner ADD MEMBER amara; ALTER ROLE db_owner ADD MEMBER barry; ALTER ROLE db_datareader ADD MEMBER clark; ALTER ROLE db_securityadmin ADD MEMBER clark; go ALTER ROLE db_owner DROP MEMBER barry; go GRANT SELECT ON OBJECT::houses TO barry; GRANT INSERT ON OBJECT::houses TO barry; go """
invoke_commandline_sql(__db_prepare__, database=None)
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database='important_data')
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT ( dp.state_desc + ' ' + dp.permission_name collate latin1_general_cs_as + ' ON ' + '[' + s.name + ']' + '.' + '[' + o.name + ']' + ' TO ' + '[' + dpr.name + ']' ) AS PermissionsForObjects FROM sys.database_permissions AS dp INNER JOIN sys.objects AS o ON dp.major_id=o.object_id INNER JOIN sys.schemas AS s ON o.schema_id = s.schema_id INNER JOIN sys.database_principals AS dpr ON dp.grantee_principal_id=dpr.principal_id WHERE 1=1 AND o.name IN ('houses') AND dp.minor_id = 0 ORDER BY dpr.name; """
__sql_verify2__ = """ SELECT ( dp.state_desc + ' ' + dp.permission_name collate latin1_general_cs_as + ' ON ' + '[' + s.name + ']' + '.' + '[' + o.name + '](' + c.name + ')' + ' TO ' + '[' + dpr.name + ']' ) AS PermissionForColumns FROM sys.database_permissions AS dp INNER JOIN sys.columns AS c ON dp.major_id=c.object_id AND dp.minor_id=c.column_id INNER JOIN sys.objects AS o ON dp.major_id=o.object_id INNER JOIN sys.schemas AS s ON o.schema_id = s.schema_id INNER JOIN sys.database_principals AS dpr ON dp.grantee_principal_id=dpr.principal_id WHERE 1=1 AND o.name IN ('houses') ORDER BY dpr.name; """
print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database='important_data')) print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify2__, database='important_data'))

questiontext.text:
Revoke `SELECT` permission on the `houses` table in the `important_data` database from user account `barry`.
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

question.answer: REVOKE SELECT ON OBJECT::houses TO barry; go

expected.text: PermissionsForObjects 1 GRANT INSERT ON [dbo].[houses] TO [barry] PermissionForColumns

extra.text:
__db_prepare__ = """ CREATE DATABASE important_data; go USE important_data; go CREATE TABLE cars ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, manufacturer VARCHAR(50), model VARCHAR(50), type VARCHAR(50), horsepower INT, ); go INSERT INTO cars VALUES (3, 'Acura' , 'ILX' , 'Sedan' , 201 ), (7, 'Fiat' , '500X' , 'SUV' , 150 ), (8, 'Lexus' , 'RC' , 'Coupe' , 467 ); go CREATE TABLE houses ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, owner_surname VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), area FLOAT, bedrooms int ); go INSERT INTO houses VALUES (2, 'Markowitz' , '293 Davis Place, Toledo, OH' , 156.2 , 3), (3, 'Earl' , '2071 Rockford Mountain Lane, Appleton, WI' , 54.5 , 1), (5, 'Hampton' , '930 Sunny Glen Lane, Cleveland, OH' , 111.9 , 2); go CREATE LOGIN amara WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN barry WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN clark WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; go ALTER SERVER ROLE dbcreator ADD MEMBER clark; go USE important_data; go CREATE USER amara FOR LOGIN amara; CREATE USER barry FOR LOGIN barry; CREATE USER clark FOR LOGIN clark; go ALTER ROLE db_owner ADD MEMBER amara; ALTER ROLE db_owner ADD MEMBER barry; ALTER ROLE db_datareader ADD MEMBER clark; ALTER ROLE db_securityadmin ADD MEMBER clark; go ALTER ROLE db_owner DROP MEMBER barry; go GRANT SELECT ON OBJECT::houses TO barry; GRANT INSERT ON OBJECT::houses TO barry; go DENY SELECT ON OBJECT::houses(address) TO barry; go """
invoke_commandline_sql(__db_prepare__, database=None)
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database='important_data')
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT ( dp.state_desc + ' ' + dp.permission_name collate latin1_general_cs_as + ' ON ' + '[' + s.name + ']' + '.' + '[' + o.name + ']' + ' TO ' + '[' + dpr.name + ']' ) AS PermissionsForObjects FROM sys.database_permissions AS dp INNER JOIN sys.objects AS o ON dp.major_id=o.object_id INNER JOIN sys.schemas AS s ON o.schema_id = s.schema_id INNER JOIN sys.database_principals AS dpr ON dp.grantee_principal_id=dpr.principal_id WHERE 1=1 AND o.name IN ('houses') AND dp.minor_id = 0 ORDER BY dpr.name; """
__sql_verify2__ = """ SELECT ( dp.state_desc + ' ' + dp.permission_name collate latin1_general_cs_as + ' ON ' + '[' + s.name + ']' + '.' + '[' + o.name + '](' + c.name + ')' + ' TO ' + '[' + dpr.name + ']' ) AS PermissionForColumns FROM sys.database_permissions AS dp INNER JOIN sys.columns AS c ON dp.major_id=c.object_id AND dp.minor_id=c.column_id INNER JOIN sys.objects AS o ON dp.major_id=o.object_id INNER JOIN sys.schemas AS s ON o.schema_id = s.schema_id INNER JOIN sys.database_principals AS dpr ON dp.grantee_principal_id=dpr.principal_id WHERE 1=1 AND o.name IN ('houses') ORDER BY dpr.name; """
print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database='important_data')) print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify2__, database='important_data'))

questiontext.text:
Change the password of the `clark` login to `NoweP@zzw0rd`.
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.
> The command will be tested from a login account that allows changing the password without knowing the current one.

question.answer: ALTER LOGIN clark WITH PASSWORD = 'NoweP@zzw0rd'; go

expected.text:
Dump from 'sys.sql_logins':
name type_desc is_disabled 1 amara SQL_LOGIN False 2 barry SQL_LOGIN False 3 clark SQL_LOGIN False
Checking users connection:
clark: ------- success
(1 rows affected)

questiontext.text:
Create the following schemas in the `university` database:
* `HumanResources` * `StudentOffice`
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

expected.text:
Dump from 'sys.schemas':
name schema_id principal_id 1 HumanResources 5 1 2 StudentOffice 6 1

extra.text:
__db_prepare__ = """USE master; CREATE LOGIN bob WITH PASSWORD = 'P@ssw0rd'; go
CREATE DATABASE university; go USE university; go CREATE TABLE student ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), surname VARCHAR(50), birthday DATE, personal_id VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), zip_code VARCHAR(10), email VARCHAR(50), tuition MONEY ); go INSERT INTO student VALUES ( 2, 'Belladonna' , 'Sandheaver', '1947-02-23', '47022329148', '3219 Poplar Lane Miami, FL', '33-176', 'BelladonnaSandheaver@jourrapide.com', 2025), ( 7, 'Fredegar', 'Greenhand', '1985-07-06', '85070644484', '3311 Laurel Lee Saint Paul, MN', '55-106', 'FredegarGreenhand@teleworm.us', 1024), ( 9, 'Diamanda', 'Goodchild', '1994-09-14', '94091438255', '1647 Rhode Island Avenue Washington, DC', '20-007', 'DiamandaGoodchild@dayrep.com', 0); go CREATE TABLE faculty ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), surname VARCHAR(50), birthday DATE, personal_id VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), zip_code VARCHAR(10), email VARCHAR(50), salary MONEY ); go INSERT INTO faculty VALUES ( 3, 'Tayla', 'Fairley', '1973-03-26', '73032676844', '1737 Central Avenue Ramsey, NJ 07446', '07-446', 'TaylaFairley@armyspy.com', 4057), ( 6, 'Evie', 'Cox', '1952-02-26', '52022678499', '3888 Kenwood Place Fort Lauderdale, FL', '33-301', 'EvieCox@dayrep.com', 11754), ( 12, 'James', 'Buchanan', '1950-05-21', '50052165886', '1176 Cook Hill Road Stamford, CT', '06-902', 'JamesBuchanan@rhyta.com', 7780); go CREATE USER bob FOR LOGIN bob; go ALTER ROLE db_datareader ADD MEMBER bob; ALTER ROLE db_datawriter ADD MEMBER bob; go """
invoke_commandline_sql(__db_prepare__, database=None)
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database='university')
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT * FROM sys.schemas WHERE name IN('HumanResources' ,'StudentOffice'); """ print("Dump from 'sys.schemas':\n") print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database='university'))

questiontext.text:
Set the owner of the `HumanResources` schema to the database user account `bob`.
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

question.answer: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON schema::HumanResources TO bob; go

expected.text: SchemaName Principal 1 HumanResources bob 2 StudentOffice dbo

extra.text:
__db_prepare__ = """USE master; CREATE LOGIN bob WITH PASSWORD = 'P@ssw0rd'; go
CREATE DATABASE university; go USE university; go CREATE TABLE student ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), surname VARCHAR(50), birthday DATE, personal_id VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), zip_code VARCHAR(10), email VARCHAR(50), tuition MONEY ); go INSERT INTO student VALUES ( 2, 'Belladonna' , 'Sandheaver', '1947-02-23', '47022329148', '3219 Poplar Lane Miami, FL', '33-176', 'BelladonnaSandheaver@jourrapide.com', 2025), ( 7, 'Fredegar', 'Greenhand', '1985-07-06', '85070644484', '3311 Laurel Lee Saint Paul, MN', '55-106', 'FredegarGreenhand@teleworm.us', 1024), ( 9, 'Diamanda', 'Goodchild', '1994-09-14', '94091438255', '1647 Rhode Island Avenue Washington, DC', '20-007', 'DiamandaGoodchild@dayrep.com', 0); go CREATE TABLE faculty ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), surname VARCHAR(50), birthday DATE, personal_id VARCHAR(50), address VARCHAR(100), zip_code VARCHAR(10), email VARCHAR(50), salary MONEY ); go INSERT INTO faculty VALUES ( 3, 'Tayla', 'Fairley', '1973-03-26', '73032676844', '1737 Central Avenue Ramsey, NJ 07446', '07-446', 'TaylaFairley@armyspy.com', 4057), ( 6, 'Evie', 'Cox', '1952-02-26', '52022678499', '3888 Kenwood Place Fort Lauderdale, FL', '33-301', 'EvieCox@dayrep.com', 11754), ( 12, 'James', 'Buchanan', '1950-05-21', '50052165886', '1176 Cook Hill Road Stamford, CT', '06-902', 'JamesBuchanan@rhyta.com', 7780); go CREATE USER bob FOR LOGIN bob; go ALTER ROLE db_datareader ADD MEMBER bob; ALTER ROLE db_datawriter ADD MEMBER bob; go CREATE SCHEMA HumanResources; go CREATE SCHEMA StudentOffice; go """
invoke_commandline_sql(__db_prepare__, database=None)
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database='university')
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT s.name as SchemaName, p.name as Principal FROM sys.schemas as s JOIN sys.database_principals AS p ON s.principal_id = p.principal_id WHERE s.name IN ('HumanResources', 'StudentOffice'); """ print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database='university'))

questiontext.text:
Drop the SQL Server login `amara`.
> Use `sqlcmd` syntax including the `go` command.

extra.text:
__db_prepare__ = """ CREATE LOGIN amara WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; CREATE LOGIN barry WITH PASSWORD = 'P@zzw0rd'; go """ invoke_commandline_sql(__db_prepare__, database=None)
invoke_commandline_sql(__student_answer__, database=None)
__sql_verify__ = """ SELECT name, type_desc FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE name IN ('amara', 'barry'); """ print(invoke_cursor_sql(__sql_verify__, database=None))
